newsSinceJun6: Two genuinely new items. (1) June 9: GDS (and Vnet) rallied intraday on reports that China is drafting a ~RMB 2 trillion (~$295B), five-year national data-center/computing-hub investment program led by the NDRC; caveat - early-stage proposal and state telcos (China Mobile/China Telecom) are expected to operate the majority of facilities, which dilutes direct GDS capture. (2) June 8 narrative refresh around GDS's own RMB 50B three-year AI data-center capex plan, record ~200MW Q1 new orders and ~340MW signed YTD, alongside a $300M preferred equity raise and persistent leverage/asset-sale-reliance concerns. Day move +3.57% (below the 5% threshold); YTD still -6.29%, so no rerating crowding penalty.

sources.label: Yahoo Finance - GDS and Vnet Rally on Reports of Major China Data Center Investment Plan (Jun 9, 2026)
Simply Wall St - GDS's RMB 50 Billion AI Data Center Bet (Jun 8, 2026)
Bamboo Works - GDS commits up to 50 billion yuan to capture booming AI data center demand
StockTitan - GDS sets 2026 AGM for June 25 (30% share issuance authority vote, Jun 2 - context only, pre-Jun 6)

verdicts.rankRationale: Genuinely new evidence since yesterday's up-move: reported ~RMB 2T national compute-network program plus GDS's own RMB 50B AI capex plan and record bookings directly de-risk the physical-deployment thesis, while a -6.3% YTD and $7.35B cap mean the rerating has not yet happened - residual alpha remains for an under-owned bottleneck name.
verdicts.bull: A state-backed RMB 2T five-year buildout plus GDS's record ~200MW Q1 orders and ~340MW signed YTD turn China AI demand into contracted capacity; the stock is still below its January level, so the catalyst is not priced.
verdicts.neutral: The national program is an early-stage proposal and yesterday's verdict already moved GDS up; today's +3.6% is a modest, partly flow-driven sympathy move that confirms rather than transforms the thesis.
verdicts.bear: State telcos are slated to operate the majority of the planned hubs, diluting GDS's capture of the policy spend, while elevated leverage, a $300M preferred raise, reliance on asset sales, and a pending 30% share-issuance authorization keep dilution and funding risk live.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Formal NDRC announcement of the national computing-hub program, or further large AI capacity orders/pre-commitments validating the RMB 50B capex plan.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Policy spend routed predominantly through state telcos and dilutive financing (preferred equity, share issuance) eroding per-share economics.
